crm\tests\ (test_exit_codes.py, test_metadata_update.py, test_optionsets.py, test_destructive_op_gate.py, test_lazy_imports.py, test_appmodule.py, test_batch.py, test_core.py — current contract assertions referenced by the proposals)

capabilities: Stable --json envelope on every command via CLIContext.emit (crm/cli.py:47-89): {ok, data?, error?, meta?}; success is {ok:true,...}, failure is {ok:false,error,meta}. data is recursively sanitized (bytes->utf-8) by _sanitize so output is always JSON-serializable.

Hard exit-code contract for loop-driving agents (ADR docs/adr/0001): 0=success, 1=operational failure (D365 server error, in-command validation, declined confirm), 2=Click usage error. emit(ok=false) raises click.exceptions.Exit(1) so failures never silently exit 0 (the historic bug the ADR fixed).

Usage errors are JSON-wrapped under --json without breaking the code contract: _JsonAwareGroup/_json_mode_active (crm/cli.py:124-195) intercept Click UsageError and render {ok:false,error} on stdout while preserving exit 2. argv is scanned (not parsed ctx) so the signal is reliable even before the root callback runs and in the REPL.

--dry-run previews the exact HTTP request without issuing it: backend short-circuits (d365_backend.py:305-313) returning {_dry_run:true, method, url, params, headers (Authorization stripped), body}. Safe mutation validation path documented for agents in SKILL.md.

--stage-only global flag (env CRM_STAGE_ONLY) forces every metadata create/update to --no-publish for batch-then-publish-once flows; sticky in the REPL (never silently cleared, crm/cli.py:319-323); combining with explicit --publish is rejected as a usage error; staged:true is recorded in meta (_resolve_publish in _helpers.py:91-107).

Two-layer destructive-op gate: (1) in-band --yes flag on every destructive verb (entity delete, async cancel, metadata delete-entity/-optionset, solution job-cancel) via _confirm_destructive, which on non-TTY EOF emits the documented {ok:false,error:'aborted by user'} (exit 1) instead of a bare Click Abort; (2) a deterministic stdlib-only Claude Code PreToolUse hook (.claude/hooks/destructive_op_gate.py wired in .claude/settings.json) that hard-blocks (exit 2 + stderr reason) any destructive crm Bash call lacking --yes, even if the model ignores the prompt.

PreToolUse hook is robust against shell evasion: splits compound commands on operators (&&, ||, |, ;, $(), backticks, newlines) even when glued to neighbors, strips leading FOO=1 env assignments, handles path-prefixed /usr/bin/crm, skips value-taking root options so a destructive verb can't hide after --profile, and refuses to count a --yes that is actually an option value. Matches by verb NAME so not-yet-shipped delete verbs (delete-attribute, delete-relationship, role/privilege verbs) are gated pre-emptively.

Bundled agent skill SKILL.md (crm/skills/) installable via `crm skill install --target copilot|claude|cursor` (or --dest) and `skill path`/`skill uninstall`; the skill explicitly instructs agents to always pass --json, check $? then read the envelope, use --dry-run before mutations, and pass --yes non-interactively.

Lazy command loading (_LazyJsonAwareGroup, crm/cli.py:198-254) imports a subcommand module only when invoked, so `crm --version` and direct one-shot commands avoid pulling requests/NTLM/prompt_toolkit — fast cold start for scripted single-shot agent calls.

Rich, machine-parseable error context: D365Error surfaces through _handle_d365_error as meta.status (HTTP) and meta.code (D365 hex code, e.g. 0x80040217); SKILL.md documents the recovery playbook (404/0x80040217 missing record, 401 auth, 403 privilege, 400 OptimisticConcurrency -> retry).

Query results carry consistent pagination/count meta (_emit_query_result in _helpers.py:259-276): entity_set, count, odata_count (@odata.count), next_link presence. Batch results carry a render_batch_summary in meta. service-document lists every entity set the agent can address.

Self-describing payload guidance for valid writes: `metadata picklist`/option-set introspection plus SKILL.md @odata.bind navigation-property guidance (PascalCase nav name, GlobalOptionSet@odata.bind needs MetadataId GUID on on-prem 9.1) so an agent can construct valid create payloads.

Zero-touch profile auto-wiring (ADR 0002): solution create-publisher/create write publisher_prefix/default_solution back into the active named profile so later metadata commands need no repeated --solution/prefix; guarded (--no-set-default, named-profile-only, never under --dry-run) and reported in the envelope (data.profile_updated).

Headless/non-interactive config: env-var or .env auto-load (D365_*/CRM_* aliases), `crm init --template` writes .env.example non-interactively, named profiles via `connection connect`, passwords never persisted (env/--password only). Impersonation and write-safety headers exposed as flags (--as-user, --suppress-dup-detection, --bypass-plugins).

Resilience knobs an agent can rely on: built-in transport retry with CRM_NO_RETRY scope helper, --no-transaction/--continue-on-error for $batch, optimistic concurrency via --if-match/etag, and --max-pages safety cap on --all pagination.
gaps: No machine-readable command/capability discovery surface: there is no `--help --json`, no `crm describe`/`introspect`, and `crm --help` text is plain Click prose. An agent cannot enumerate commands, options, arguments, or the option-set/choices programmatically — it must rely on the bundled SKILL.md prose or out-of-band knowledge. The lazy-command registry (_lazy_commands) is the only structured list and isn't exposed.

No MCP server. The only first-class agent integration is shell invocation + the copied SKILL.md; there is no tool-call interface, so agents must shell out and parse stdout, and the destructive gate is Claude-Code-specific (a generic agent shelling out gets no PreToolUse protection — only the in-band --yes/non-TTY abort).

Inconsistent dry-run envelope shape: a dry-run returns a raw {_dry_run, method, url, headers, body} dict as `data` with no meta.dry_run flag and no normalization across commands; an agent must sniff the `_dry_run` key inside data rather than a stable top-level/meta signal. Multi-step ops (batch, paginated export, publish) don't all model dry-run.

Exit-code granularity is intentionally flat (ADR 0001): every operational failure is exit 1 regardless of class (auth 401 vs not-found 404 vs concurrency 412 vs declined-confirm). An agent must parse meta.status/meta.code to branch — fine for --json callers but offers no signal to non-JSON or exit-code-only loops.

No structured warnings/notices channel: solution-resolution warnings are stuffed into meta.warning only under --json (_emit_with_warning) and printed as skin text otherwise; there is no consistent meta.warnings[] array, so non-fatal advisories are easy for an agent to miss.

REPL is the default with a bare `crm` invocation (drops into interactive prompt). An agent that omits a subcommand hangs on the REPL prompt instead of getting a usage error — SKILL.md warns about this but the CLI itself provides no non-interactive guard / no `CRM_NO_REPL`-style escape.

No idempotency / request-id / correlation token surface for retried mutations: --if-match covers optimistic concurrency on update/delete, but creates have no client-supplied idempotency key, so an agent retrying a create after an ambiguous transport failure risks duplicates (only the env-default duplicate-detection toggle exists).

No streaming/progress for long-running ops (solution import, large data export, --all pagination): output is emitted once at completion, so an agent gets no incremental signal and must rely on the async list/get/cancel commands polled manually.
No shell-completion registration (no _CRM_COMPLETE / shell_complete), so neither humans nor completion-aware tooling can tab-discover entity sets, profiles, or option values.
constraints: NTLM (Windows Integrated) only; OAuth/IFD/Claims/certificate auth explicitly out of scope (README, SKILL.md hard constraints).
Targets D365 CE on-premises 9.x only (v9.0/9.1/9.2); Dataverse online auth differs and is unconfigured.
Live D365 server is a hard runtime dependency — no local mocking; E2E tests fail loudly without D365_URL.

Exit codes 0/1/2 are now a committed public contract (ADR 0001): changing them is a breaking change; usage errors must stay 2 (never reclassified to 1), and failure-class detail must live in the JSON envelope, never in new exit codes.
Passwords are never persisted to disk — only via D365_PASSWORD/CRM_PASSWORD env or --password; profiles store no secret.

pyright strict on crm/core/* and crm/utils/d365_backend.py (basic elsewhere); core modules must stay pure D365 with a mockable backend and must not import session/config or touch ~/.crm (per ADR 0002 — config writes live only in the command layer).
--dry-run must have zero side effects, including no profile writes (ADR 0002 guards both ctx.dry_run and the result's _dry_run sentinel).

The PreToolUse destructive gate is a Claude-Code-specific mechanism (.claude/settings.json + stdlib hook, exit 2 blocks); it protects only agents running under Claude Code's hook system, not arbitrary shell-out agents.
The single source of truth for top-level command exposure is the _lazy_commands map in crm/cli.py — a new top-level command is invisible unless registered there.
Sticky REPL flags (profile/password/stage-only) are deliberately not cleared on a later bare line to preserve safety/connection state; agents reusing one REPL session inherit prior root-option state.

Solution browsing + job control — `solution list` (managed filter), `solution info`, `solution components` (componenttype/objectid/rootcomponentbehavior), `solution job-status` and `solution job-cancel` (aliases over async ops). crm/core/solution.py:190,201,212

Workflow/process ops (core surface) — list_workflows (filter by category/primaryentity/activated/on-demand, definition records only), set_workflow_state (activate/deactivate via statecode/statuscode PATCH), execute_workflow (ExecuteWorkflow bound action). crm/core/workflow.py — note these ARE surfaced via crm/commands/workflow.py (present in tree, outside the requested file set).

Staging / ALM safety model — global `--stage-only` flag forces every mutating command to --no-publish (changes land in the solution unpublished for a later batched publish; explicit --publish + --stage-only is rejected). `--solution`/`--require-solution` (or CRM_REQUIRE_SOLUTION) target MSCRM.SolutionUniqueName with precedence explicit > profile default_solution > none + warning. --if-exists error|skip idempotency on every create. Schema names auto-derived from profile publisher_prefix + PascalCase display token. Universal --dry-run preview path (forced-real existence GETs, merged-PUT body + diff preview). crm/commands/_helpers.py:91,147,192
OData surface introspection — `metadata list-actions` / `metadata list-functions` parse $metadata CSDL to enumerate service-declared actions/functions. crm/core/metadata.py:385,390
gaps: No forms (systemform) authoring — cannot create or edit main/quick-create/quick-view forms or form XML. Forms can only be *referenced by GUID* via `app add-components` (kind=form/dashboard); the GUID must be obtained out-of-band. No `form` command group exists.
No charts/visualizations (savedqueryvisualization) authoring — same as forms: bindable by GUID in `app add-components` (kind=chart) but not creatable/editable by the CLI.
No dashboard creation — dashboards are systemform records; bindable by GUID only, no authoring command.
No business process flow (BPF) authoring — `app add-components` can bind a BPF by workflowid (kind=bpf) but there is no command to create/configure a BPF.

No business rules / classic workflows / actions / modern-flow authoring — workflow.py only lists, activates/deactivates, and on-demand-executes existing workflows (categories enumerated as constants); no create/update of any process category.
Attribute deletion — no `delete-attribute`/`delete-column`; only entity and global-option-set deletion exist. An attribute (or lookup/relationship) created in error cannot be removed via the CLI.
Relationship deletion — no `delete-relationship`; create-one-to-many and create-many-to-many exist but no removal path (relationships also block entity deletes, with no CLI remedy).

View update/delete — `view create` only; no edit (columns/fetchxml/layoutxml) or delete of an existing savedquery. Only public views (querytype 0) are creatable; no user/personal views, lookup views, quick-find, or associated views.
App update/delete — `app create` only; no rename/edit/delete of an appmodule, and no `remove-components` to undo an AddAppComponents binding.

Sitemap is create-only and write-only — `app set-sitemap` POSTs a new sitemaps row from raw XML; no read/update of an existing sitemap and the operator must hand-author SiteMapXml (no subarea/group builder).
Publisher/solution: no update or delete — cannot bump solution version, change friendlyname/description, delete a solution, or delete/edit a publisher via the CLI (create-only).

No managed-solution uninstall/upgrade path — import handles ImportSolutionAsync but there is no StageAndUpgrade/ApplySolutionUpgrade/HoldingSolution flow nor a delete/uninstall command; the delete pre-flights even point users to 'uninstall the parent solution' with no CLI verb to do so.

No solution-component add/remove — cannot add an existing component to a solution (AddSolutionComponent) or remove one (RemoveSolutionComponent) after the fact; components only enter a solution at create-time via the MSCRM.SolutionUniqueName header. `solution components` is read-only.
No web resource / plugin / SDK message processing step management — cannot create or update web resources (icons reference a hard-coded default GUID), register plugin assemblies, or manage SDK steps.
No keys / alternate keys, no calculated/rollup field authoring — add-attribute covers base typed columns only; no EntityKey creation and no FormulaDefinition/rollup configuration.
No solution clone / patch (CloneAsPatch, CloneAsSolution) and no SetSolutionVersion.
No security-role / field-security-profile / column-level-security customization.
constraints: Target is D365 CE on-premises v9.x (Dataverse Web API OData v4 over HTTPS + NTLM); on-prem-specific workarounds are baked in: global option set @odata.bind requires MetadataId GUID (Name key rejected on 9.x), appmodule must publish before read-back (9.1), and ExportSolutionAsync falls back to synchronous ExportSolution on older orgs. crm/core/metadata_attrs.py:361, appmodule.py:104, solution.py:224

Metadata updates have no PATCH — Dataverse replaces the whole definition on PUT. The CLI always GETs the full current definition, deep-merges a sparse changes dict, and PUTs the complete body with MSCRM.MergeLabels:true to avoid silently dropping omitted/localized properties. Typed attributes must be read/written through their @odata.type cast path. crm/core/metadata_update.py:1

Schema/logical-name rules enforced client-side before HTTP: schema names must include a publisher prefix and be PascalCase; logical name is the lowercased schema name. Names can be auto-derived from the active profile's publisher_prefix + PascalCase display token. crm/core/metadata.py:177, _helpers.py:192

Delete operations are guarded: refuse non-custom (IsCustomEntity/IsCustomOptionSet=False) or managed (IsManaged=True) targets client-side; the server still enforces dependency checks (workflows/forms/relationships) and returns 4xx. Destructive verbs require interactive confirm or --yes. crm/core/metadata.py:300, optionsets.py:237

Publish defaults ON for create/update commands (PublishAllXml after the write) but is globally suppressed by --stage-only so changes can be batched and published once; combining explicit --publish with --stage-only is a usage error. crm/commands/_helpers.py:91

Solution targeting is via the MSCRM.SolutionUniqueName request header at create-time only (precedence: --solution > profile default_solution > none-with-warning; --require-solution / CRM_REQUIRE_SOLUTION makes the absence a hard failure). There is no post-hoc add-to-solution. crm/commands/_helpers.py:147

Read-back after create is best-effort and non-fatal: a failed read-back (e.g. unparseable OData-EntityId, GET error) records a *_lookup_error in the result rather than failing the create. crm/core/metadata.py:259

Granular optionset updates are NOT transactional: insert→update→delete→reorder run sequentially and stop on first error, leaving prior steps applied (completed_steps + stage attached to the raised D365Error; no rollback). crm/core/optionsets.py:151

Async solution export/import block until completion or a timeout (profile.async_timeout, overridable per-invocation via --timeout); a per-invocation --no-retry disables the 429/5xx retry loop. crm/core/solution.py:268,356

N:N relationships cannot be self-referential (Entity1==Entity2 rejected) and have no CascadeConfiguration/AssociatedMenuConfiguration — cascade/menu updates are only valid for 1:N. crm/core/relationships.py:240, metadata_update.py:475
Views assume the primary-id attribute is `<entity>id` (true for custom tables, which is the intended target) and only create public views (querytype 0). crm/core/views.py:54

App component binding via AddAppComponents only accepts record-backed components (view/chart/form/dashboard/sitemap/bpf as typed entity references); tables are not crmbaseentity records and must reach the app through the sitemap's Entity= subareas instead. crm/core/appmodule.py:31

capabilities: Viable AI path = a generic coding agent (Claude Code or GitHub Copilot CLI/agent-mode) acting as ORCHESTRATOR — runs shell, edits files, spawns executables, and attaches arbitrary STDIO/HTTP/SSE MCP servers. This is the only architecture that reaches on-prem.

This repo's `crm` CLI + `crm` skill IS the bridge: real Dataverse Web API (OData v4) over HTTPS + NTLM, implemented in `crm/utils/d365_backend.py` (Python NetworkCredential + HttpClientHandler pattern). Supports record CRUD, OData/FetchXML queries, metadata browsing, and `solution import`/`solution export`.

The `crm` CLI already ships the core agent-safety guardrails (codebase audit 2026-05-30): `--json` deterministic output (`ctx.emit` envelope), `--dry-run` request preview, and `solution publish` / `publish-all` commands. These guardrails are DONE.

Solution import/export over Web API confirmed: `ImportSolution` (CustomizationFile base64, OverwriteUnmanagedCustomizations, PublishWorkflows, returns ImportJobId), `ExportSolution` (ExportSolutionFile inline base64), and `AddSolutionComponent` (unmanaged-only, 6 params) are real OData v4 actions in `Microsoft.Dynamics.CRM`, stable across v9.0/v9.1/v9.2. The `crm` CLI already ships the commands — no new CLI command needed.

Classic on-prem SDK actuators confirmed via `Microsoft.CrmSdk.CoreTools` NuGet (9.1.0.179, `bin\coretools`): SolutionPackager.exe (pack/extract managed+unmanaged solutions for source control) and CrmSvcUtil.exe (early-bound codegen — the documented on-prem replacement for pac). Agent spawns these as ordinary processes.

On-prem customization surface is fully metadata-driven and Microsoft-supported: custom tables/columns/option sets, 1:N/N:1/N:N relationships, forms/views/charts/dashboards/apps/sitemaps, business process flows, classic workflows, actions, business rules. Code extensibility via plug-ins, custom workflow activities, Client API JS + web resources, webhooks, Azure extensions, async service.
On-prem-only capability: full-trust plug-ins running in the same app domain (not sandbox-only like cloud), with on-disk assembly storage at `<installdir>\Server\bin\assembly`.

Two Microsoft-DOCUMENTED-for-on-prem agent-safety primitives exist: (1) solution-aware in-context create/update via the `MSCRM.SolutionUniqueName` request header (Microsoft explicitly prefers this over standalone AddSolutionComponentRequest) — validates issue #22; (2) `ImportJobId` + `RetrieveFormattedImportJobResults` / querying the ImportJob table for verifiable import outcomes.
Microsoft Learn MCP works on-prem as a docs-grounding aid (not an automation actuator). Playwright MCP is a viable last-resort fallback to drive the D365 web UI when no API path exists (brittle).

Recommended end-to-end pipeline: (1) AUTHOR via `crm` CLI Web API/NTLM, (2) GENERATE C#/JS + early-bound types via CrmSvcUtil.exe, (3) TEST via MSBuild + integration tests through `crm` CLI, (4) PACKAGE via SolutionPackager.exe, (5) DEPLOY via synchronous Web API ImportSolution (`crm solution import`), (6) VERIFY by re-querying via `crm` CLI.
gaps: Q3 (STILL OPEN, 🔴, primary unanswered question): on-prem metadata Web API limits/throttling under NTLM at v9.0/v9.1 — whether page-size/payload caps, unsupported metadata ops, or cloud-style service-protection throttling (HTTP 429, x-ms-ratelimit, Retry-After) apply on-prem. No verified evidence either way. Working assumption: cloud 429 is a multi-tenant mechanism likely absent on-prem, but MUST be verified/field-measured against the actual target server before tuning batch size/concurrency.

Async solution staging on-prem unestablished: `StageSolution` -> `ImportSolutionAsync` -> poll asyncoperation is a cloud-era construct. The asyncoperation polling entity is DOC-ON-PREM, but StageSolution + ComponentParameters (env vars / connection refs) on-prem availability is NOT established. Until verified, use synchronous ImportSolution (+ ExecuteAsync).

PackageDeployer + Configuration Migration Tool + Plugin Registration Tool bundling in CoreTools NuGet is NOT confirmed (the claim that CoreTools bundles the full legacy set was refuted 0-3). Treat them as separate downloads. Configuration Migration on-prem exists but its packaging is uncertain.
Plugin Registration Tool is GUI-first → likely an unavoidable semi-manual pipeline step unless spkl / xrm-ci-framework fully scripts plugin-assembly registration for v9.x (unconfirmed).

No on-prem MCP server exists. Open question whether any OSS MCP does NTLM/IFD against the Web API — `oisee/odata_mcp_go` (generic OData v4 MCP) is a 🟡 candidate worth a spike (NTLM support unconfirmed); otherwise every team must wrap the `crm` CLI. An MCP wrapper of `crm` was considered and DROPPED (SKILL.md + --json already make it agent-usable); revisit only if Copilot-side MCP integration is required.

Copilot coding agent (cloud) cloud->on-prem-LAN network reach is untested (the two negatives — cloud-ephemeral, single-repo-locked — were REFUTED 0-3, so do not over-constrain, but practical LAN access is an open practical question).
Behavioral differences between /api/data/v9.0, /v9.1, /v9.2 endpoints on the same on-prem server are unverified.

Most Part-3 agentic-safety patterns (dry-run metadata diff, publish gating, retrieve-merge-write for PUT, destructive-op guards) are ENG-JUDGMENT, NOT Microsoft-documented — the agentic angle produced ZERO independently-verified claims. They are valid engineering hardening (issues #18-#21), not vendor-prescribed.

Open hardening issues tracked: #18 idempotent create (--if-exists skip|error), #19 --stage-only safe mode, #20 update-entity/attribute/relationship with retrieve-merge-write, #21 destructive-op PreToolUse hook + confirm coverage, #22 default solution + publisher prefix warning.
constraints: NO turnkey AI path reaches on-prem v9.x. Every first-party + popular community Dataverse-AI tool is architecturally cloud-only (Entra ID auth, *.crm.dynamics.com URLs, Power Platform Admin Center / Managed Environments). None work on an IFD/NTLM box. Everything cloud-native is dead-on-arrival on-prem.

pac CLI is NOT supported on-prem (DEFINITIVE, verbatim op-9-1: 'the Power Platform CLI is not available for Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement (on-premises), you must use CrmSvcUtil.exe'). pac auth offers only Entra-ID/Azure cloud auth — no NTLM/AD/AD FS/IFD path. No Power Platform Build Tools on-prem. Deploy MUST go via Web API ImportSolution, never pac.

Cloud-only and explicitly UNAVAILABLE on-prem: Dataverse Skills plugin, Dataverse MCP server (URL hard-coded to https://{org}.crm.dynamics.com/api/mcp), pac built-in MCP, Copilot Studio / Copilot for Power Platform, mwhesse/dataverse-mcp (OAuth2 client-creds only). Also no maker portal, no admin center, no managed environments, no Solution Checker, no Power Automate cloud flows.

On-prem auth = EXACTLY THREE security models: (1) Claims-based, (2) Active Directory / NTLM (NetworkCredential + HttpClientHandler — what this repo uses), (3) OAuth 2.0 but IFD-only (requires AD FS 3.x+). Cloud Dataverse OAuth-via-Entra does NOT apply. Doc warning: do NOT use ServiceClient when using AD FS for on-prem auth.

Metadata UPDATE is PUT-replace, NEVER PATCH (the single most important safety fact). PUT replaces the ENTIRE definition; you must include all existing properties you don't intend to change. Any automated metadata update MUST retrieve-then-merge the full current definition first or it SILENTLY WIPES properties. Carve-outs: option-set options use dedicated UpdateOptionValue; business data rows do use PATCH normally.

Publish is a separate platform-enforced gate: after updating a table/column definition, changes are NOT applied until PublishXml / PublishAllXml Web API action is called — a natural stage-vs-production boundary.
Create-custom-table requires a StringAttributeMetadata primary-name attribute with IsPrimaryName: true in the POST body to EntityDefinitions.

Security privileges are CUMULATIVE (union of all roles) and effectively irreversible-by-accident — an over-broad role can't be 'un-unioned' by adding another. 8 record-level privileges x 5-tier access hierarchy (Global/Org > Deep > Local/BU > Basic/User > None). Treat role/privilege grants as irreversible-class ops.
AddSolutionComponent targets UNMANAGED solutions ONLY. Use synchronous ImportSolution on-prem (StageSolution/ImportSolutionAsync are cloud-era and not established on-prem).

Order of trust for guardrails (never let the weakest be the only layer): platform constraints (free, strongest) -> CLI validation/dry-run -> Claude Code PreToolUse hooks (deterministic block) -> prompt instructions (weakest). The platform's own constraints ARE the safety rails — encode them, don't invent new ones.

Business-rule scope gotcha: under Entity scope only Set Field Value, Set Default Value, Show Error Message run server-side; Lock/Unlock, Set Visibility, Set Business Required, Recommendation are model-driven-app only and silently ignored server-side.

Time-sensitivity: all 'online-only' verdicts are accurate as of 2026-06-03 only — MS AI tooling is brand-new (Dataverse Skills/MCP = 2026, pac built-in MCP = Dec 2025, Copilot CLI GA = Feb 2026); Microsoft may add on-prem support later, so re-check. Metadata facts are 🟡 cloud-pathed inferences (auth-independent OData v4 contract, only version path differs) — not direct op-9-1 statements.

Do NOT repeat these refuted myths: 'four clean classic process types' (refuted 0-3); 'OAuth is cloud-only/undocumented for on-prem' (refuted — it IS documented but gated to IFD+AD FS); 'Copilot coding agent is cloud-ephemeral-only' and 'locked to a single GitHub.com repo' (both refuted 0-3).

refuted.title: `solution scaffold` to source-control prep: write solution.xml/Other/Customizations skeleton for SolutionPackager
refuted.note:
The `solution scaffold` command does NOT exist in the repo (solution subcommands are: list, info, components, create-publisher, create, export, publish-all, publish, job-status, job-cancel, import — crm/commands/solution.py:43-265). SKILL.md:296 "Solution scaffolding" refers to live publisher+solution CREATION, not a folder layout. So this is non-existent, but it is NOT a genuine gap worth filling, because the proposal is technically misguided for D365 CE on-prem v9.x.

The core claim — that a hand-written minimal skeleton (Other/Solution.xml + empty Other/Customizations.xml + folder shell) is "the exact directory SolutionPackager.exe expects to pack" — is REFUTED by the authoritative op-9-1 docs the finding itself cites:

1. SolutionPackager [s17] /folder arg: "When packing, this folder must already exist and contain PREVIOUSLY EXTRACTED component files." Pack is the inverse of Extract; it reassembles a .zip from files SolutionPackager itself produced (incl. [Content_Types].xml, component subfolders, content hashes) — not a hand-fabricated stub.

2. The op-9-1 "Use source control with solution files" doc states verbatim: "manual editing of extracted component files and .zip files is not supported" (except the narrow Customizations.xml sections in when-edit-customization-file). Generating Solution.xml/Customizations.xml from scratch is outside the supported surface.

3. The documented "Create a solution" source-control procedure is: create in org -> EXPORT unmanaged solution -> SolutionPackager EXTRACT -> add files to source control. The folder skeleton is PRODUCED by export+extract, never hand-scaffolded. The grounding doc's own AUTHOR step says the agent "EDITS solution XML + customizations" (edits files that exist from an extract), not generates them.

The actual on-prem ALM facts (pac not available on-prem; SolutionPackager is THE source-control tool; synchronous ImportSolution) are accurate and well-grounded (onprem-automation.md:143,269-279; ai-agent-tooling-onprem.md:105-135). But the proposed solution misreads the SolutionPackager contract: the org-side half of the prep (export the .zip) is ALREADY shipped as `crm solution export`; the only remaining step is invoking SolutionPackager.exe /action:Extract against that .zip — a documented external one-liner, not a folder the CLI should fabricate. A scaffolded stub would not round-trip through pack and would mislead an agent into an unsupported workflow.
